perturbation.original_text: e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3(e) mandates the return of all Confidential Information. The added sentence 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of Confidential Information for archival purposes' creates a contradiction. The Receiving Party is simultaneously obligated to return all copies and permitted to keep one, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This contradiction is created inside Section 3(e).
perturbation.contradicted_text: e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 3(e) requires the return of *all* Confidential Information. The changed version, by adding 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of Confidential Information for archival purposes,' directly contradicts the original requirement to return *all* copies. The Receiving Party is simultaneously obligated to return all copies and permitted to keep one, creating uncertainty. The changed text breaks compatibility with the original requirement of returning all confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm; however, the ownership of such inventions will be determined on a case-by-case basis, considering the Receiving Party's contribution.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7 states that all Inventions are the exclusive property of Vapotherm. By adding 'however, the ownership of such inventions will be determined on a case-by-case basis, considering the Receiving Party's contribution,' the exclusive ownership of Vapotherm is contradicted. This addition introduces uncertainty as to who owns the inventions. This contradiction occurs within Section 7, where the last sentence 'All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm' is being modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm; however, the ownership of such inventions will be determined on a case-by-case basis, considering the Receiving Party's contribution.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 7 explicitly states that all inventions are the exclusive property of Vapotherm. The changed text introduces a contradictory statement that ownership will be determined on a case-by-case basis. This directly contradicts the original statement of exclusive ownership, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the same Section 7. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', because the changed text creates conflicting implications within the same section, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, demonstrating that this modification to the same section does create a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Survival. The restrictions and obligations of this Agreement shall continue and shall survive the termination of the Purpose of the disclosure for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Survival. The restrictions and obligations of this Agreement shall continue and shall survive the termination of the Purpose of the disclosure. The duration of this survival clause is subject to annual review and may be terminated at Vapotherm's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Section 11 originally states that the obligations survive for five years. By removing the five-year term and adding 'The duration of this survival clause is subject to annual review and may be terminated at Vapotherm's discretion,' a contradiction is introduced. The original clause specifies a fixed survival period while the modified text allows for discretionary termination. This creates uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality. This change is made within Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Survival. The restrictions and obligations of this Agreement shall continue and shall survive the termination of the Purpose of the disclosure. The duration of this survival clause is subject to annual review and may be terminated at Vapotherm's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year survival period. The changed text removes the five-year term and replaces it with a clause allowing Vapotherm to terminate the survival clause at its discretion after annual review. This directly contradicts the original fixed survival period, introducing uncertainty and creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the obligations and restrictions, which makes it a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made in writing and signed by each party.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made in writing and signed by each party. However, Vapotherm reserves the right to unilaterally modify specific clauses related to data usage, with notification to the Receiving Party within 30 days of such modification.
perturbation.explanation: Section 17 requires written agreement for any modifications. The added sentence 'However, Vapotherm reserves the right to unilaterally modify specific clauses related to data usage, with notification to the Receiving Party within 30 days of such modification' creates a contradiction by allowing Vapotherm to unilaterally modify the agreement, which undermines the original requirement of mutual agreement. The specific location where the contradiction arises is in Section 17 itself, due to the additive nature of the perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Modification. No addition to, deletion from, or modification of the provisions of this Agreement shall be permitted or shall be binding upon a party hereto unless made in writing and signed by each party. However, Vapotherm reserves the right to unilaterally modify specific clauses related to data usage, with notification to the Receiving Party within 30 days of such modification.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 17 states that any modification requires a written agreement signed by all parties. The changed text adds a sentence that allows Vapotherm to unilaterally modify specific clauses related to data usage. This directly contradicts the original requirement of mutual agreement for all modifications, creating a clear conflict within the same section, as the contradicted text is the same section (Section 17) as the Changed Text. The perturbation explanation clearly defines the contradiction.
